definition: The place someone calls home. This is usually a permanent place that they live and could be any type of shelter or habitat.
sentence: The fox returned to its warm abode.

definition: A thought of something that does not physically exist and cannot be referenced by a concrete object.
sentence: The painting used abstract shapes and colours.

definition: A large amount of something that is more than you require.
sentence: Spring brought an abundance of bright flowers.

definition: Celebrate or give praise to someone in a public environment.
sentence: The singer earned acclaim after the concert.

definition: A way of honouring an achievement of a person or a place.
sentence: She received an accolade for kindness.

definition: To go with or alongside something or someone.
sentence: I will accompany Mum to the shop.

definition: To reach a goal or successfully reach a finishing point.
sentence: You can achieve your goal with practice.

definition: Despite not wanting to agree to something, you would rather not accept.
sentence: He chose to acquiesce and stay calm.

definition: To share information to someone in a spoken or written medium.
sentence: The teacher will address the noisy class.

definition: Sticking to something - this could be a thought or belief or something physically sticking to something else.
sentence: We adhere to the rules at school.

definition: Very nearby or next to each other.
sentence: Our classroom is adjacent to the library.

definition: Stating that something is true.
sentence: Your ticket allows admission to the museum.

definition: To advise against something and warn a person you do not think it is wise.
sentence: Dad may admonish us for shouting.

definition: To take something and follow or use it as your own.
sentence: They plan to adopt a small puppy.

definition: Something that can have a negative or unfavourable impact on an event.
sentence: Rain had an adverse effect on sports day.

definition: Showing anger or carrying out something in a forceful way.
sentence: The aggressive dog barked at strangers.

definition: To make someone feel uneasy.
sentence: The loud noise began to agitate her.

definition: To give or provide help.
sentence: Volunteers aid families after the storm.

definition: A conjunction that could be used in place of although.
sentence: The walk was fun, albeit very muddy.

definition: To attract or charm someone or something.
sentence: The beach had the allure of adventure.

definition: Someone who is new to an event or takes part as a hobby rather than a profession.
sentence: The amateur painter kept practising every day.

definition: Something that has a nice smell or taste.
sentence: The ambrosial cake smelled fresh and sweet.

definition: A large amount that is adequate or more than adequate.
sentence: We had ample time to finish.

definition: Something that is old and out-of-date - it might need modernising.
sentence: The antiquated clock still ticked softly.

definition: Having little interest or a lack of emotion towards something.
sentence: His apathy showed during the team talk.

definition: To understand the value of something and be aware that it is there.
sentence: I appreciate your help with homework.

definition: Feeling nervous or cautious about something as you fear something bad might happen.
sentence: She felt apprehensive before the spelling test.

definition: Something that is hard and strenuous and might leave you feeling tired.
sentence: Climbing the hill was an arduous task.

definition: A disagreement involving two or more different viewpoints that oppose each other.
sentence: Their argument ended when they listened.

definition: Dry land with no rain. This land is so dry that it would not be able to support plant life.
sentence: The arid land needed plenty of rain.

definition: Clever and intelligent in what is being completed.
sentence: Her artful plan solved the puzzle.

definition: Being able to speak and express ideas clearly and coherently.
sentence: The articulate speaker explained every step.

definition: A person skilled in a trade or craft that requires them to work with their hands.
sentence: The artisan made bowls from clay.

definition: Feeling embarrassed or guilty about something you have done or said.
sentence: He felt ashamed after breaking the vase.

definition: Collect things or people into one place. You could assemble a group of people or objects.
sentence: We assemble the model after lunch.

definition: To divide or separate something into multiple pieces.
sentence: The strong wind blew the papers asunder.

definition: To try or make an effort to do something that is difficult.
sentence: Her first attempt was nearly correct.

definition: A sign of things to come in the future. This could be good or bad.
sentence: Dark clouds may augur heavy rain.

definition: Reduced spending and living without things as you increase frugality.
sentence: The family practised austerity to save money.

definition: A strong dislike towards something and the feeling of wanting to move or turn away from it.
sentence: He has an aversion to soggy cereal.

definition: To not go near someone or something and to stay away.
sentence: Try to avoid puddles in new shoes.

definition: Being welcoming and kind to someone who might have less experience than you or is younger.
sentence: The avuncular coach made everyone feel welcome.

definition: Being cruel and unsophisticated in the way you are acting.
sentence: The barbaric act shocked the village.

definition: Nothing out of the ordinary - something very normal and boring.
sentence: The story felt banal and predictable.

definition: Due to or for this reason.
sentence: We stayed inside because it was raining.

definition: A gesture to direct someone or get them to do as you wish.
sentence: She used her hand to beck him closer.

definition: Something that is suitable and has a nice appearance.
sentence: The blue scarf looked becoming on her.

definition: A person or thing connected or collaborating with someone else on a specific project.
sentence: The campers joked about a snoring bedfellow.

definition: A hostile attitude that might result in fighting or arguments.
sentence: The belligerent player argued with everyone.

definition: To be unhappy about something and to express that grief.
sentence: They bemoan the loss of the old tree.

definition: Something that gives an advantage or has a positive impact and can help you out.
sentence: Fresh air is a benefit of camping.

definition: Something that has no negative effect and is not harmful.
sentence: The benign old dog wagged slowly.

definition: Not being loyal and sharing information with an enemy or opposition.
sentence: Never betray a friend for attention.

definition: Deeply confused and unable to decide what the right choice is.
sentence: I looked bewildered by the strange map.

definition: Having a preference and acting in an unfair way due to this.
sentence: The report seemed biased and incomplete.

definition: Extremely happy with no sadness or discontent.
sentence: We had a blissful afternoon at the lake.

definition: A mistake that has been made due to a lack of care or consideration.
sentence: Spilling the paint was a blunder.

definition: To offer support and strengthen someone or something.
sentence: Good notes bolster your memory before tests.

definition: A display of courage intended to impress another person.
sentence: His bravado disappeared during the race.

definition: Well-developed muscles and lots of physical strength.
sentence: Moving the sofa took plenty of brawn.

definition: A manner of speech that can seem sharp and come across as impolite.
sentence: His brusque reply hurt her feelings.

definition: A hiding place to keep a collection of items safe.
sentence: The squirrel found its nut cache.

definition: A disastrous problem that causes distress and misfortune.
sentence: The flood was a great calamity.

definition: An unplanned and honest expression.
sentence: Please be candid about your mistake.

definition: A person who is nominated for or applies for a position.
sentence: Each candidate gave a short speech.

definition: A job which a person has trained for and progresses through during their life.
sentence: She dreams of a career in science.

definition: A charming person who compels other people.
sentence: The charismatic leader inspired the team.

definition: A pleasing and pleasant quality.
sentence: The cottage looked charming in spring.

definition: When something has been badly burnt and reduced to charcoal.
sentence: The charred toast smelled smoky.

definition: To convince or persuade someone to do something they do not wish to.
sentence: No one should coerce others to cheat.

definition: Something that is clear and logically organised.
sentence: Her answer was coherent and easy to follow.

definition: Not a formal style of language but one that is conversational and might be specific to a local area.
sentence: He used colloquial words with friends.

definition: Joining two or more things together.
sentence: The snack was a combination of fruit and nuts.

definition: Something that is funny and provides people with amusement.
sentence: The comical clown tripped over big shoes.

definition: To remember and show respect for a person or event.
sentence: We commemorate heroes on special days.

definition: Pressuring or forcing someone to take a certain action.
sentence: Strong evidence may compel a confession.

definition: Difficult and challenging often with lots of connected parts.
sentence: The puzzle looked complex at first.

definition: Obeying an order and acting within given guidelines.
sentence: The class showed compliance with the rules.

definition: A trusted friend who you have things in common with such as a job or hobbies.
sentence: My comrade shared his lunch with me.

definition: Having a high self-opinion and being too proud of your abilities.
sentence: The conceited boy bragged all day.

definition: The end or finale of something.
sentence: We reached a conclusion after the discussion.

definition: Proving something is certainly true and ending any doubt around a scenario.
sentence: The clue gave conclusive proof at last.

definition: Agreeing with another person and having the same opinion.
sentence: I concur with your sensible idea.

definition: Not approving of something and criticising it strongly.
sentence: Leaders condemn cruelty wherever they see it.

definition: To overlook and accept a behaviour or action that might be bad or illegal.
sentence: Good teachers never allow bullying.

definition: Providing the conditions to make an outcome certain or highly likely.
sentence: Quiet music is conducive to calm reading.

definition: Taking something away from someone when you have the right to do so.
sentence: The teacher confiscated the noisy toy today.

definition: An idea or opinion that is formed without solid evidence.
sentence: My answer was only a conjecture.

definition: Permission from all parties involved to partake in an activity.
sentence: We asked Mum for consent first.

definition: The outcome of an event as a result of something else.
sentence: It rained hard; consequently, football was cancelled.

definition: Taking time to think about something before making a judgement or decision.
sentence: Your kind consideration made everyone feel included.

definition: To plot with others to secretly take an action that is wrong or illegal.
sentence: The villains conspire in the secret cave.

definition: To use something.
sentence: Plants consume water and sunlight each day.

definition: Something that can spread and move easily between people.
sentence: Yawning can be strangely contagious in class.

definition: Exists in the current period of time.
sentence: The gallery showed contemporary paintings and sculptures.

definition: Items that differ greatly in an obvious way.
sentence: The contrast between colours looked striking.

definition: Feeling apologetic for behaviour you know was not appropriate.
sentence: He felt contrite after breaking the vase.

definition: A difficult and confusing problem or puzzle.
sentence: Choosing one prize was quite a conundrum.

definition: A person who has been found guilty of a crime.
sentence: A jury may convict after hearing the facts.

definition: Being warm and polite towards another person in a social situation.
sentence: Our new neighbours gave us a cordial welcome.

definition: Longing for something and having a strong desire to have it.
sentence: Do not covet someone else's shiny bicycle.

definition: Acting in a shy and reserved manner.
sentence: The coy kitten hid behind the sofa.

definition: Offensive in manner and showing no sensitivity toward a situation or other people.
sentence: His crass joke upset the whole table.

definition: Attribution of someone's efforts towards something.
sentence: Ella earned credit for her hard work.

definition: A deceitful character who plans to get what they want.
sentence: The cunning fox stole the farmer's lunch.

definition: To restrain or stop something from happening.
sentence: We must curb littering around the playground.

definition: Being interested and eager to learn something new.
sentence: The curious child opened the science book.

definition: Speaking briefly and coming across in a rude manner.
sentence: His curt reply ended the conversation quickly.

definition: Something extremely bright and very beautiful. Something so bright that it can temporarily blind a person.
sentence: The dazzling fireworks lit up the sky.

definition: Discuss opposing arguments on a specific topic in a formal way.
sentence: We debate ideas during our history lesson.

definition: To gradually go on a downwards trend.
sentence: I must decline another slice of cake.

definition: Clearly defined without any ambiguity.
sentence: There is a definite answer to this.

definition: To go against an order and do something you have been told not to or that was unexpected.
sentence: The hero will defy the unfair ruler.

definition: Feeling disheartened and not having much hope.
sentence: Bad news can deject even brave players.

definition: A very large amount of something - often water - causing a flood.
sentence: A deluge soaked the streets after lunch.

definition: To break something down or completely destroy it.
sentence: Workers will demolish the old shed safely.

definition: To have knocked something down or completely destroyed it.
sentence: The storm demolished the small wooden fence.

definition: To act in a reserved and shy manner.
sentence: She gave a demure smile to guests.

definition: To represent or show something by a visual image.
sentence: The mural will depict our town's history.

definition: To stop or suspend an activity.
sentence: Please desist from tapping your pencil now.

definition: Extremely poor and lacking things such as food and shelter.
sentence: The story told of a destitute traveller.

definition: To change gradually and build or grow.
sentence: Practice helps you develop strong reading skills.

definition: To come up with something such as a plan.
sentence: We will devise a clever team plan.

definition: To destroy completely to the point where nothing is left at all.
sentence: The puppy tried to devour the sandwich.

definition: The choice of words when speaking and the manner in which they are pronounced.
sentence: Clear diction helps everyone understand your speech.

definition: To break something down.
sentence: Take time to digest the new facts.

definition: Stray from the main topic of conversation or writing and discuss something else.
sentence: Do not digress during your short talk.

definition: Temporarily stray from the main topic of conversation or writing.
sentence: His funny digression delayed the story.

definition: Showing care in your work and working consistently with effort to achieve something.
sentence: The diligent pupil finished every task neatly.

definition: Has been weakened or thinned by something else.
sentence: The diluted juice tasted very light.

definition: Not bright and the levels of brightness might be insufficient.
sentence: The dim torch barely lit the path.

definition: To reduce and to become less.
sentence: Shadows diminish as the sun rises.

definition: Damage the reputation and refuse to believe it as being true.
sentence: False rumours can discredit a good person.

definition: Untidy and very messy appearance.
sentence: After football, his hair looked dishevelled.

definition: Not having interest in something as it doesn't feel like you will benefit from it.
sentence: A disinterested judge listens to both sides.

definition: Gloomy and sad with little hope.
sentence: The dismal weather kept us indoors.

definition: Spread out in different directions and scatter widely.
sentence: The crowd began to disperse after lunch.

definition: Connected with a god.
sentence: The painting showed a divine light above.

definition: Can be easily persuaded or controlled.
sentence: The docile lamb followed the farmer quietly.

definition: Asserting your own opinions as facts.
sentence: His dogmatic tone stopped the discussion early.

definition: Relating to home - whether this is a property, family or country.
sentence: The cat is a domestic animal.

definition: Slowed down and temporarily inactive.
sentence: The volcano stayed dormant for many years.

definition: A quantity or portion of something to be given.
sentence: The doctor gave one small dose.

definition: Lacking certainty and not certain to be true.
sentence: I am doubtful about the cloudy forecast.

definition: Very serious and sullen.
sentence: The dour guard rarely smiled at visitors.

definition: Very harsh and severe.
sentence: The draconian rules upset many students.

definition: Obediently doing as you should and motivated to do so by a sense of duty.
sentence: The dutiful helper cleaned up after art.

definition: Prioritising your own desires and having an emphasised sense of self-importance.
sentence: His egoistical bragging annoyed the whole team.

definition: The basic and introductory elements of a topic.
sentence: This puzzle seems elementary to solve.

definition: Speaking clearly with fluency and aptness.
sentence: Her eloquence impressed the whole audience.

definition: Explaining something to make it easier to understand.
sentence: The teacher will elucidate the tricky maths.

definition: A ban or order to prevent something from happening.
sentence: The country placed an embargo on trade.

definition: Hold someone or something in your arms tightly.
sentence: We should embrace new ideas in class.

definition: A large group or organisation being run by one person.
sentence: The ancient empire stretched across many lands.

definition: Copy the behaviour of another in an attempt to equal them or excel.
sentence: Young players often emulate their sporting heroes.

definition: Publicly approve or give support for something.
sentence: Many teachers endorse reading every evening.

definition: Keep going without stopping even when something is difficult or unpleasant.
sentence: We can endure the hike together.

definition: A project or business being undertaken.
sentence: Their lemonade stand became a fun enterprise.

definition: Have great interest in and be excited about something.
sentence: The coach tried to enthuse the team.

definition: The whole amount of something with nothing left out.
sentence: She read the entire book in bed.

definition: A sudden realisation or moment of understanding.
sentence: I had an epiphany during science class.

definition: Not speaking clearly and deliberately using vague language.
sentence: Do not equivocate when asked a question.

definition: A period of time that is marked by distinctive character or a prominent figure.
sentence: Dinosaurs lived in a different era.

definition: Assemble something into a vertical position.
sentence: Workers will erect a new playground fence.

definition: A lack of regularity and can be unpredictable.
sentence: The erratic wind moved the kite wildly.

definition: Used to single something out and make it stand out.
sentence: I love fruit, especially sweet strawberries.

definition: Controlling yourself in a polite way and following the social rules.
sentence: Good etiquette matters at the dinner table.

definition: Overstate something and make it sound better or worse than it really is.
sentence: Do not exaggerate the size of fish.

definition: Sudden emotion of surprise or horror.
sentence: The crowd began to shout with joy.

definition: Make it easier for someone to complete an action.
sentence: Good signs help ease our journey.

definition: Something that can be proven to be true and is known to exist or to have happened.
sentence: It is a fact that plants need water.

definition: Extreme lack of food - not enough for the people in the area.
sentence: The drought caused hunger across the land.

definition: Something that is extremely dangerous and can cause great harm.
sentence: Wearing a helmet helps keep us safe.

definition: A fake item that is made to look like something more valuable.
sentence: She wore a fake fur hat.

definition: Physically weak and lacking in strength.
sentence: The feeble puppy needed extra care.

definition: Pretend or fake an illness or appearance to deceive someone.
sentence: He tried to pretend he was asleep.

definition: Something humiliating that fails completely.
sentence: The play rehearsal turned into a mess.

definition: An imaginative idea that seems real but is not.
sentence: The monster was just a fantasy.

definition: A metaphorical idea that is not based on a literal meaning.
sentence: The poem used symbolic language about hope.

definition: Attracts attention due to being striking and confident.
sentence: His colourful jacket stood out everywhere.

definition: Giving lots of praise as you want to get something from someone.
sentence: Her kind words made him smile.

definition: An imperfection in something, such as blemish or a fault.
sentence: The flawed plan needed more thought.

definition: Something that is perfect and has no faults or problems.
sentence: Her spelling test was perfect today.

definition: To run away or to escape.
sentence: The rabbit ran from the loud noise.

definition: A group of vehicles owned by an organisation.
sentence: The fleet horse won the race.

definition: Not made with substance and can be easily broken.
sentence: The flimsy box tore apart quickly.

definition: A disrespectful attitude that doesn't take things seriously.
sentence: His cheeky reply upset the teacher.

definition: Plants that can be found in a particular place.
sentence: The island's plants were bright and varied.

definition: To grow healthily and thrive.
sentence: Sunflowers thrive in warm sunshine.

definition: A competitor or opponent.
sentence: The hero faced his enemy bravely.

definition: An ancestor who lived in the past.
sentence: My ancestor lived in this village.

definition: Lonely and feeling separation from loved ones.
sentence: The lost dog looked sad and lonely.

definition: The basis that something is built upon.
sentence: Strong bricks make a solid base.

definition: A small piece of something that has broken off from the whole.
sentence: A piece of glass sparkled outside.

definition: Delicate and easily broken.
sentence: The frail bird needed gentle hands.

definition: Acting in an unorganised way and emotionally uncontrolled.
sentence: Mum felt panicked when keys vanished.

definition: Deceiving a person for your own gain.
sentence: The email was a clever scam.

definition: Acting in a way that is intended to trick someone.
sentence: Police stopped the fake ticket sale.

definition: Happening regularly at short intervals.
sentence: We have regular library visits on Fridays.

definition: Something which causes intense fear.
sentence: The storm made a terrible noise.

definition: The foremost part of a building, person or object.
sentence: Please stand at the front of the line.

definition: Careful with money and live without unnecessary items.
sentence: She was careful with her pocket money.

definition: An amount of money saved for a specific purpose.
sentence: The class raised money for books.

definition: Something that is necessary - an essential basis or foundation.
sentence: Reading is a basic school skill.

definition: To cause a person to suddenly take action through shock or excitement.
sentence: The speech inspired the team to act.

definition: A piece of clothing.
sentence: She folded each piece of clothing neatly.

definition: A way of measuring or calculating an amount.
sentence: We can measure the jar with a ruler.

definition: Something that has gold, or something that looks like gold on its surface.
sentence: The frame had a shiny golden edge.

definition: Speaking without thinking and often without honesty despite speaking confidently.
sentence: His easy answer lacked real thought.

definition: A poorly lit space that is dim.
sentence: Rain made the park look dark.

definition: Eating too much regularly and being excessively greedy.
sentence: The greedy eater finished all the biscuits.

definition: Something that is impressive in size and very grand.
sentence: The castle had great splendour inside.

definition: Break something down by rubbing two things together.
sentence: Please scrape the cheese for dinner.

definition: Someone who is well liked and enjoys the company of others.
sentence: The friendly boy made friends quickly.

definition: A comically strange and unpleasant character.
sentence: The mask looked strange and ugly.

definition: Very enthusiastic and energetic.
sentence: She was eager to start the project.

definition: A place that a person or thing calls home.
sentence: A pond is a frog's home.

definition: To stop moving or going somewhere.
sentence: The guard told everyone to stop.

definition: To prevent or restrict movement for others.
sentence: Heavy snow can slow the buses.

definition: A physically attractive person.
sentence: He wore a smart suit today.

definition: To repeatedly put pressure on or bother a person resulting in them being unhappy.
sentence: It is wrong to bully classmates.

definition: To listen carefully and give something your attention.
sentence: Please listen to the warning sign.

definition: Acting in an unorganised manner and doing something in a rush.
sentence: The papers flew about chaotically.

definition: Acting bravely and showing determination.
sentence: The brave firefighter rescued the cat.

definition: Arranged in an order of hierarchy to reflect importance.
sentence: The club had a ranked team system.

definition: An organisational system that ranks things in terms of their importance.
sentence: The school chart showed the order clearly.

definition: Recognising and understanding something once it has already happened.
sentence: In reflection, I should have packed earlier.

definition: Something that is fake and has been set up to deceive people.
sentence: The fake alien photo was a prank.

definition: A break from the usual routine where you can do as you please.
sentence: We went to the beach on holiday.

definition: To shout or call out loudly.
sentence: Do not shout inside the library.

definition: Having compassion and kindness towards others.
sentence: Kind people care for injured animals.

definition: What you think might happen but you don't yet have the evidence to support this.
sentence: We discussed an imaginary trip to Mars.

definition: Being inactive or not being used.
sentence: The engine stayed still in the garage.

definition: Someone or something that is admired and respected.
sentence: The singer became her pop idol.

definition: Something that is beautiful and peaceful with little threat.
sentence: The farm looked peaceful at sunset.

definition: Something that is perfect and has no flaws.
sentence: Her room was spotless after cleaning.

definition: To be completely involved in something.
sentence: I like to absorb myself in books.

definition: Unresponsive and not showing any emotion towards what has happened.
sentence: She stayed calm during the surprise.

definition: Preventing a task from progressing or being completed.
sentence: Mud can slow our walking pace.

definition: Urgent and requires immediate attention.
sentence: It is essential to wash your hands.

definition: Not good enough or lacking in quality.
sentence: One torch was insufficient for the cave.

definition: A cut in or through something caused by a sharp instrument.
sentence: The doctor made a small cut.

definition: A surface that is sloping upwards.
sentence: The ramp began to slope gently upward.

definition: Deemed low in importance and has little significance.
sentence: That mistake was small and fixable.

definition: A person that holds a public office or an official post.
sentence: The current captain led the team.

definition: What makes a person them, e.g. facts about them or personality traits.
sentence: Your identity makes you unique.

definition: Something that is unfair or when bias has been seen.
sentence: The teacher fixed the unfairness quickly.

definition: Lacking strength and being unable to move.
sentence: The toy lay still on the shelf.

definition: Something that happens to or is suitable for a child.
sentence: His childish joke annoyed the class.

definition: Has a lower status or is deemed less important than something else.
sentence: This copy is poorer than the original.

definition: Breathing inwards, taking air into your lungs.
sentence: Breathe in slowly before the dive.

definition: Will cause no harm and is unlikely to upset anyone.
sentence: The small insect was harmless to us.

definition: A state of mind that is not determined to be sound.
sentence: The plan sounded insane to everyone.

definition: To suggest something but not directly say it.
sentence: She did not insinuate anything unkind.

definition: Something that is abstract and cannot be specifically described.
sentence: Hope is intangible but still powerful.

definition: To get involved in a situation you do not need to be part of.
sentence: Please do not interfere with our game.

definition: To cause a break and stop an activity.
sentence: Do not interrupt when someone is speaking.

definition: To overwhelm and provide a large amount of things at the same time.
sentence: Heavy rain can inundate the fields.

definition: Something having the opposite result of what was expected and causing humour.
sentence: It was ironic that the lifeguard slipped.

definition: Cut off from others and completely alone.
sentence: The cub felt sad in isolation.

definition: Terminology specific to a community that wouldn't make sense out of context.
sentence: The science jargon confused the class.

definition: Studying the law.
sentence: She reads about jurisprudence in big books.

definition: To collapse or overturn.
sentence: The boat's keel helps it stay steady.

definition: Hard and physical work.
sentence: Their labour built the new playground.

definition: A confusing maze made up of passages that are hard to navigate.
sentence: We wandered through the garden labyrinth.

definition: Lacking enthusiasm and having a lack of interest.
sentence: His lackadaisical walk slowed the group.

definition: An often secluded place where a wild and potentially dangerous animal lives.
sentence: The fox hid in its lair.

definition: Expressing sorrow, grief or mourning.
sentence: They lament the loss of the old tree.

definition: Very still and lacking in mental or physical energy.
sentence: The cat gave a languorous stretch.

definition: Towards or closer to the end of something than the beginning.
sentence: Of the two, I chose the latter.

definition: Extravagant and more than is needed.
sentence: They planned a lavish party indoors.

definition: Having legal responsibility for something.
sentence: You are liable for your own mess.

definition: Inaccurate and damaging information that has been written about a person.
sentence: The newspaper was sued for libel.

definition: Being free to make your own decisions and not under control of anyone else.
sentence: The hero came to liberate the village.

definition: Acting how you want and having the freedom to do so.
sentence: Liberty lets people speak their minds.

definition: In place of something else.
sentence: We used juice in lieu of milk.

definition: A body part that comes from the body, e.g. an arm or leg.
sentence: The bird sat on a tree limb.

definition: To hang around in a place for a long period.
sentence: Please do not linger after the bell.

definition: Settling an argument between two people by taking it to court.
sentence: The company faced litigation after the accident.

definition: The words that make up a language.
sentence: The poem uses rich lexis and rhythm.

definition: A small habitat where you stay temporarily.
sentence: We will lodge our complaint tomorrow.

definition: High up and tall.
sentence: The eagle nested on a lofty cliff.

definition: Something that is questionable and not very moral or reliable.
sentence: They avoided the louche alley at night.

definition: Supporting and being faithful to something regardless of circumstance.
sentence: A loyal friend stands by you.

definition: Something that relates to or resembles the moon.
sentence: We watched the lunar eclipse together.

definition: Being kind or forgiving towards an enemy or competitor.
sentence: She was magnanimous after winning the race.

definition: Something that strongly attracts something else.
sentence: His magnetic smile made everyone grin.

definition: A person who violated the law by carrying out illegal activity.
sentence: The malefactor tried to escape quietly.

definition: Acting in a cruel way and wishing harm or evil on another.
sentence: The story had a malevolent witch.

definition: Avoiding work by exaggerating or pretending you have an illness.
sentence: He tried to malinger to miss practice.

definition: A small amount - very little in quantity.
sentence: The squirrel found a meagre meal.

definition: Winding and aimless course.
sentence: We watched the river meander slowly.

definition: Expressing an intention a person had.
sentence: I meant to call after school.

definition: A very small amount which is too small for purpose.
sentence: They offered a measly prize today.

definition: Gentle and soft natured. Willing to go along with others and will not disagree.
sentence: The meek puppy hid behind me.

definition: Something that sounds nice and follows a tune.
sentence: The bird sang a melodious tune.

definition: Showing compassion that leads to forgiveness.
sentence: The judge showed mercy to the boy.

definition: Emphasising how small and simple something is.
sentence: It was a mere scratch.

definition: Very precise and taking a lot of care.
sentence: She was meticulous with her homework.

definition: To tease or laugh at someone or something.
sentence: Do not mock someone for mistakes.

definition: Not using colour - only shades of black and white.
sentence: The old photo looked monochrome and faded.

definition: The standards you have of what is right and wrong.
sentence: It was the moral choice.

definition: Ordinary and will not live forever.
sentence: Every mortal person needs food and rest.

definition: Inspiration for a person who is creating art.
sentence: She would muse beside the window.

definition: A very high quantity.
sentence: A myriad of stars filled the sky.

definition: Ordered and in good condition.
sentence: Keep your desk neat each day.

definition: An essential requirement for a specific result.
sentence: Water is necessary for all plants.

definition: Something that can't be defeated and is always one step ahead.
sentence: The hero finally faced his nemesis.

definition: A place of peace away from all suffering.
sentence: The quiet beach felt like nirvana.

definition: A small cost.
sentence: We paid a nominal fee to enter.

definition: New to an activity and has not experienced it before.
sentence: As a novice, she asked many questions.

definition: A promise, often made in law, to tell the truth.
sentence: He made an oath to help.

definition: Disagreeing and having a differing opinion.
sentence: She raised an objection during the meeting.

definition: On a slant or angle.
sentence: He gave an oblique glance away.

definition: Not sharp or alert with understanding.
sentence: The obtuse answer missed the point.

definition: To predict a challenge and remove it in advance.
sentence: Good planning can obviate many problems.

definition: Burdening a person or group of people and making them feel uncomfortable.
sentence: No one should oppress weaker people.

definition: Seeking to impress people with your wealth and using this to attract attention.
sentence: His ostentatious coat sparkled in sunlight.

definition: Obvious and not concealed away.
sentence: Her overt smile showed real joy.

definition: Something right in front of you that can be perceived.
sentence: The excitement was palpable in the hall.

definition: Heartbeat that is not regular. Faster than usual and usually due to excitement or nerves.
sentence: My heart can palpitate before a test.

definition: Noisy situation with no order.
sentence: Pandemonium broke out after the whistle.

definition: To act sensitively towards someone and give them what they want.
sentence: Good teachers should not pander to whining.

definition: Something that seems true but cannot be possible.
sentence: The sentence sounded like a paradox.

definition: Two things running alongside each other staying the same distance apart.
sentence: The two lines are parallel.

definition: Reword something using less words or in your own words.
sentence: Please paraphrase the passage in class.

definition: A humorous style of work.
sentence: The play was a funny parody.

definition: Strict with money - unwilling to spend a large amount.
sentence: The parsimonious man counted every coin.

definition: Different to the norm and can be seen as strange.
sentence: That peculiar sound came from the attic.

definition: Thinking deeply and carefully about something.
sentence: She looked pensive by the pond.

definition: To walk leisurely with no rush and no destination.
sentence: We perambulate around the park daily.

definition: To lie when giving a testimony under oath in court.
sentence: Perjury can get a witness punished.

definition: Completely confused and puzzled by something.
sentence: I felt perplexed by the tricky riddle.

definition: The way someone is perceived based on how they present themselves. This is usually different from their true character.
sentence: His stage persona was loud and funny.

definition: A serious sickness that spreads quickly and makes many people ill.
sentence: Stories told of pestilence in old towns.

definition: Something of little significance and isn't very important.
sentence: Their petty argument wasted the afternoon.

definition: Acting in a childish way and being irritated by something.
sentence: The petulant child stomped away angrily.

definition: Beautiful to look at and looks like art.
sentence: We painted the picturesque village by hand.

definition: One of the first people to do something.
sentence: The pioneer built homes on new land.

definition: An important and critical part of something that determines the outcome.
sentence: Her speech was pivotal to the plan.

definition: Very calm and laid back.
sentence: The lake looked placid at sunrise.

definition: Not very exciting and happens a lot.
sentence: His speech was full of tired platitudes.

definition: Something agreeable that has an enjoyable outcome.
sentence: The garden had a pleasing smell.

definition: A promise of something.
sentence: She made a pledge to help.

definition: A bad situation that is often unpleasant and can be dangerous.
sentence: We learnt about the farmer's plight.

definition: To move something quickly and forcibly into something else.
sentence: The bird will plunge into water.

definition: A sensible solution acting reasonably and logically.
sentence: She made a pragmatic choice quickly.

definition: Shocking news or behaviour that seems completely unreasonable.
sentence: That excuse sounds preposterous to everyone.

definition: Displaying self-importance and having an elevated view of yourself over others.
sentence: The pompous speaker bragged for hours.

definition: To think about something and consider it carefully.
sentence: I like to ponder tricky questions.

definition: Has lots of small holes that liquids and gases can pass through.
sentence: Porous rock soaks up rainwater fast.

definition: To present information as a fact or something that is assumed to be true.
sentence: Scientists posit ideas and test them.

definition: Behaving in a sensible way after thinking things through practically.
sentence: Her practical shoes were good for hiking.

definition: To talk for a long time in a childish way makes little sense.
sentence: The toddlers prattle during snack time.

definition: Being ahead of others for your age group developmentally.
sentence: The precocious girl read long books.

definition: An animal that kills and eats other animals for survival.
sentence: The predator stalked quietly through grass.

definition: Having an opinion that might not be true without gaining all of the facts.
sentence: Prejudice can hurt people deeply.

definition: Showing little respect by overstepping boundaries that have been set.
sentence: It was presumptuous to answer for her.

definition: A claim or false appearance to make people believe something.
sentence: His smile was only a pretence.

definition: To get or obtain something from someone else.
sentence: We can procure wood for the project.

definition: To declare something as true.
sentence: He would profess loyalty to the team.

definition: Money that is left over from a business after all the money you spent or been deducted.
sentence: The shop made a small profit.

definition: The amount of something in comparison to the whole.
sentence: Only a small proportion joined the club.

definition: A short sentence or couple of sentences that share a story of advice.
sentence: My grandma shared a wise proverb.

definition: To cause someone to behave in an angry way.
sentence: Do not provoke the sleeping dog.

definition: Acting carefully and thoughtfully.
sentence: It was prudent to save money.

definition: Not private and can be accessed by anyone.
sentence: The park is open to public use.

definition: A small hole in something made by something sharp.
sentence: A thorn can puncture a bicycle tyre.

definition: A normal and regular example of something.
sentence: Tea feels quintessentially British to me.

definition: To shake due to emotions.
sentence: The puppy began to quiver with fear.

definition: A gentle walk for pleasure - often walking around aimlessly.
sentence: We ramble through woods on Sundays.

definition: The result of an event and the actions you might have to take due to this.
sentence: Every choice has a ramification later.

definition: Searching through a place roughly and leaving it very messy.
sentence: Thieves tried to ransack the old house.

definition: Something that is uncommon and does not happen or appear often.
sentence: A rare bird landed near us.

definition: Signing something to agree with the terms set out.
sentence: Leaders met to ratify the agreement.

definition: Very hungry and needing to eat soon.
sentence: After football, I felt ravenous.

definition: Speak something out loud from memory without using any notes.
sentence: She can recite the poem clearly.

definition: A cylinder shape that is used for storing string.
sentence: He began to reel after spinning.

definition: Denying and being unwilling to accept or participate in something.
sentence: I refuse to cheat in tests.

definition: To repeat information that has been given to you.
sentence: Some birds regurgitate food for chicks.

definition: To love doing something and enjoy the experience.
sentence: I relish reading by the window.

definition: A small part of something that is left over and unused.
sentence: A remnant of cake stayed behind.

definition: To reject something and forcefully back away from it.
sentence: Strong smells can repel some insects.

definition: To be sorry and apologetic about something you have done.
sentence: He chose to repent and change.

definition: Disappointed in someone and blame them for a negative outcome.
sentence: Mum may reproach me for lying.

definition: Words that sound similar due to their ending sounds.
sentence: We wrote a rhyme about spring.

definition: A long and drawn out process that is complicated to complete.
sentence: The form-filling was a silly rigmarole.

definition: To aimlessly wander from place to place.
sentence: Cows roam across the green field.

definition: Dishonest and unpredictable.
sentence: The rogue stole pies from windowsills.

definition: A loud and boisterous environment or person.
sentence: The rowdy crowd shouted all afternoon.

definition: Instructions that are given about how something should be done.
sentence: The rubric explained how marks were given.

definition: An area away from built up areas like the city and more of a countryside environment.
sentence: They moved to a rural village.

definition: A holy object that should be respected.
sentence: The temple held a sacred statue.

definition: To give something up for something else.
sentence: She would sacrifice time to help.

definition: Sensible and of a healthy mind.
sentence: We made a sane choice together.

definition: Using words in a humorous way to mock something.
sentence: His sarcasm made the room awkward.

definition: Mocking someone by using words in a humorous way to mean the opposite of what you are saying.
sentence: Her sarcastic tone hurt his feelings.

definition: Beautiful to look at and often natural.
sentence: We took the scenic route home.

definition: To earn a point in a game or competition against another person or team.
sentence: Our team scored twice before lunch.

definition: Take an opportunity either forcibly or with excitement.
sentence: The eagle tried to seize the fish.

definition: Not drawing attention to yourself and acting modestly.
sentence: The self-effacing winner thanked her team.

definition: Understanding the meaning of something.
sentence: They argued about semantics, not ideas.

definition: An angel held in high regard.
sentence: The painting showed a bright seraph.

definition: Being calm and peaceful without stress or worry.
sentence: The garden filled us with serenity.

definition: A religious speech often with sections of the Bible.
sentence: The sermon taught kindness and patience.

definition: Disorganised and disastrous.
sentence: Our first rehearsal was totally shambolic.

definition: Mischievous behaviour often to deceive someone.
sentence: Their playground shenanigans made us laugh.

definition: A place devoted to someone who is respected.
sentence: We visited a quiet mountain shrine.

definition: To get smaller in size.
sentence: Wool jumpers can shrink in hot water.

definition: Something that is important and noticeable to others.
sentence: Winning the prize was significant to her.

definition: Comparing one thing to another using the words 'like' or 'as'.
sentence: We learnt a simile in class today.

definition: A reputation or something or someone with something else.
sentence: The toy castle was a simulacrum.

definition: Honest and with genuine feelings or emotion.
sentence: He gave a sincere apology afterwards.

definition: To loosen something and make it less intense.
sentence: Do not slacken your grip now.

definition: Speak negatively about someone and damage their reputation. The information given is usually untrue.
sentence: Spreading slander can deeply hurt others.

definition: Informal type of language that can be specific to an area or group of people.
sentence: Teenagers often invent new slang words.

definition: Something that has a smooth and good appearance.
sentence: The sleek dolphin leapt from water.

definition: Acting in a cunning and deceptive manner.
sentence: The sly fox hid behind bushes.

definition: Lonely and isolated with little socialisation.
sentence: She enjoyed solitude beside the lake.

definition: Having a deep sadness.
sentence: The sombre music made us quiet.

definition: A 14 line verse written in a particular pattern and rhythm.
sentence: She wrote a sonnet about spring.

definition: Plant seeds to grow and produce food.
sentence: We sow seeds in the garden.

definition: Not something you would see every day and has a greater purpose.
sentence: Today feels special to our class.

definition: Acting in a nasty way to upset or hurt another person.
sentence: His spiteful words upset the team.

definition: An argument about something unimportant.
sentence: The twins squabble over tiny things.

definition: Living conditions that are dirty and unpleasant.
sentence: No child should live in squalor.

definition: A pool of water that is standing still and not flowing.
sentence: The stagnant pond smelled very bad.

definition: Someone's position or view on something.
sentence: Her stance on fairness was clear.

definition: A paragraph or group of lines in a poem.
sentence: Each stanza had four short lines.

definition: Something that is staying still and not having any changes.
sentence: The picture stayed static on screen.

definition: The current situation and the way things are.
sentence: They wanted to keep the status quo.

definition: Never showing your feelings.
sentence: She stayed stoical during the storm.

definition: To bend over and have slouched head and shoulders.
sentence: Please stoop under the low branch.

definition: Something that is thick and is very strong.
sentence: The stout table held many books.

definition: Something that is pulled to its limit and can't handle any more pressure.
sentence: The cat stretched across the warm rug.

definition: Set in their way and unwilling to try anything other than what they want.
sentence: My stubborn mule would not budge.

definition: Something that exists out of sight under the earth's surface.
sentence: Subterranean tunnels ran under the hill.

definition: Straight to the point and clear in meaning.
sentence: Her answer was succinct and smart.

definition: To give in to something and stop resisting.
sentence: Do not succumb to peer pressure.

definition: Something that is good enough and will be enough for the purpose.
sentence: We had sufficient food for everyone.

definition: The top of or the highest part.
sentence: They reached the summit by noon.

definition: Thinking you are above others and acting in a way that reflects this.
sentence: His supercilious smile annoyed the team.

definition: Something that is excessive and is a lot more than is required.
sentence: That extra button seemed superfluous there.

definition: Better than something else or of higher status.
sentence: This tool is superior to mine.

definition: To overcome a challenge you have been having.
sentence: We can surmount this hard challenge.

definition: A person who is thought to have done something wrong, but it has not been proven.
sentence: I suspect the answer is wrong.

definition: A small stirring sound, like a whisper.
sentence: We heard a susurration in leaves.

definition: A person that flatters those of higher status to try and gain from them.
sentence: The king trusted every sycophant nearby.

definition: A mark or sign that represents something else.
sentence: A dove is a symbol of peace.

definition: Something that has two matching halves.
sentence: The butterfly showed perfect symmetry.

definition: A small amount of something bad in something else.
sentence: Lies can taint a good friendship.

definition: Running slow and arriving later than expected.
sentence: Ben was tardy for music practice.

definition: Pulled tight.
sentence: The rope was taut across the gap.

definition: The desire to do something you know you shouldn't.
sentence: The cake was a strong temptation.

definition: Hesitant to do something as you are unsure.
sentence: She gave a tentative answer first.

definition: Suddenly push something in a specific direction.
sentence: He thrust the door open quickly.

definition: The day that follows the day you are currently on.
sentence: We will visit Grandma tomorrow afternoon.

definition: Peaceful and calm. A place where you will not be disturbed.
sentence: The lake looked tranquil at dawn.

definition: Goes beyond the normal limits and exceeds everything you thought possible.
sentence: Her music felt transcendent and bright.

definition: Small bits of information that are not important.
sentence: The quiz asked about movie trivia.

definition: As the sun is setting just before it gets completely dark.
sentence: We walked home in the twilight.

definition: A ruler who leads in their own way without law and often in a cruel way.
sentence: The tyrant made unfair rules daily.

definition: Seeming to appear everywhere at the same time.
sentence: Mobile phones are ubiquitous today.

definition: Having a lack of interest and concern towards something.
sentence: His uncaring reply hurt her feelings.

definition: Something that is regular and always remains the same.
sentence: The rows looked uniform and neat.

definition: Joining a group of things together to make one.
sentence: The union of teams helped everyone.

definition: Something that applies to everyone everywhere.
sentence: Smiling is a universal sign.

definition: Something that is different to the traditional or expected way of doing things.
sentence: Her unorthodox idea solved the puzzle.

definition: Giving something but not having it returned.
sentence: His unrequited crush soon faded away.

definition: Something unexpected and unfortunate.
sentence: Nothing untoward happened on the trip.

definition: Acting with determination and persevering when something is difficult.
sentence: She fought valiantly for her team.

definition: Small drops of liquid that appear as a cloud.
sentence: Warm vapour rose from the kettle.

definition: Very big.
sentence: The desert looked vast and empty.

definition: Make a prediction about what is going to happen in the future.
sentence: Some people vaticinate the weather badly.

definition: A thin piece of material used to cover the face.
sentence: A thin veil covered the moon.

definition: The circulation of fresh air.
sentence: Good ventilation keeps classrooms comfortable.

definition: A boat or a ship that is large in size.
sentence: The old vessel crossed the bay.

definition: Experiencing things through another person.
sentence: I felt vicarious joy watching them.

definition: Very unpleasant.
sentence: The rubbish gave off a vile smell.

definition: A female fox.
sentence: A vixen hid near the trees.

definition: Something that is very large and bulky.
sentence: She wore a voluminous winter coat.

definition: Feeling tired either mentally or physically.
sentence: After hiking, we felt weary.

definition: Something unusual and different to what is expected.
sentence: That weird noise came from outside.

definition: Something that is playful and unpredictable. Often impractical.
sentence: The artist drew whimsical flying cats.

definition: Persistently complaining about something small.
sentence: The whining child wanted more sweets.

definition: Something good for you that can improve your life.
sentence: We packed a wholesome lunch today.

definition: Intentionally carrying out a damaging act.
sentence: His wilful choice caused more trouble.

definition: Longing for something from the past.
sentence: She gave a wistful look outside.

definition: Being sharp and clever with words.
sentence: His wit made the whole class laugh.

definition: Drying up and disappearing.
sentence: Plants wither without enough water.

definition: A witty comment.
sentence: Her witticism made everyone chuckle.

definition: Being quick and clever with words for a humorous effect.
sentence: He gave a witty reply.

definition: Having value and being deserving of something.
sentence: She is worthy of the prize.

definition: An open injury to your body.
sentence: He cleaned the wound carefully.

definition: A boat that is often used for sailing.
sentence: A white yacht sailed past us.

definition: The amount of produce that is created.
sentence: Apple trees yield fruit in autumn.

definition: Having a lot of enthusiasm.
sentence: She gave a zestful dance performance.

definition: The opinions and beliefs people have at a specific period in time.
sentence: That song captured the zeitgeist well.
